user_story: As an analyst I check whether a case is password-protected and whether I have already unlocked it
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/lock-status returns {hasPassword, unlocked, remembered}; 400 invalid caseId; 404 unknown case; unlock state derived from signed cookie verification
test_result: security review + 18/18 tests/server/casePasswordRoutes.test.ts passing (incl. new traversal test)
errors: Medium: route skipped isValidCaseId, unlike sibling password routes -> crafted caseId reached store.getCaseMeta before existence check (path traversal, narrow exploitability)
fix: added isValidCaseId(id) guard + regression test in casePasswordRoutes.test.ts

user_story: As an analyst I unlock a password-protected case to view it
expected_behaviour: POST /cases/:id/unlock verifies password (scrypt + timingSafeEqual) against stored salted hash; 400 invalid caseId; 401 wrong password; issues signed HttpOnly SameSite=strict cookie (1yr TTL if remember:true, else 12h session); no-op 200 if case has no password
test_result: security review (hashing/timing-safe/HMAC/cookie flags all cleared) + 18/18 tests passing; path-traversal guard now covered by regression test
errors: Medium (fixed): same isValidCaseId gap as lock-status. High/Medium open (deployment-dependent, NOT auto-fixed): no rate limiting or brute-force lockout on /unlock -- unthrottled guessing possible if server is ever bound beyond 127.0.0.1 (DFIR_HOST=0.0.0.0)
fix: path-traversal: isValidCaseId guard added. Rate limiting: NOT applied -- needs a policy decision (lockout threshold/duration, per-IP vs per-case) before implementing; recommend express-rate-limit or a per-case attempt counter on /unlock

user_story: As an analyst I set or change a case password to restrict access
expected_behaviour: POST /cases/:id/password sets/changes password; 400 if below MIN_CASE_PASSWORD_LENGTH or invalid case id; 404 unknown case; does not auto-unlock the setter's own browser

user_story: As an analyst I remove password protection from a case
expected_behaviour: DELETE /cases/:id/password clears the password and the unlock cookie; same 400/404 validation as set

user_story: As an analyst I re-lock a case on this browser without changing the password
expected_behaviour: POST /cases/:id/lock clears only the local unlock cookie; always 200, no case-existence check, idempotent

user_story: As an analyst I restore an archived case back to active
expected_behaviour: POST /cases/:id/restore moves case out of _archived/, sets status to closed; 400 if not currently archived

user_story: As an analyst I permanently delete a finished case, optionally archiving it first
expected_behaviour: POST /cases/:id/delete requires status closed/archived (400 otherwise); body {archiveFirst: none|zip|encrypted, password?}; encrypted path requires password >= MIN_PASSWORD_LENGTH, streams .dfircase binary with X-Case-Deleted header; a successful archive is preserved even if the subsequent delete step fails

user_story: As an analyst I export a case as a password-protected encrypted archive
expected_behaviour: POST /cases/:id/export/encrypted body {password, removeFromList?} produces an AES-256-GCM .dfircase zip; 400 if case already archived or password too short

user_story: As an analyst I import a .dfircase encrypted archive to restore or transfer a case
expected_behaviour: POST /cases/import/encrypted body {data (base64), password, targetCaseId?}; 409 on case id conflict, 400 on wrong password or corrupt archive; sanitizes any embedded case-lock password before responding

user_story: As an analyst I recover a case from an automatic snapshot after data loss or a bad edit
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/backups lists snapshots; POST /cases/:id/restore-backup body {filename} restores one; 404 if backupManager unconfigured or case missing

user_story: As an analyst I track long-running background jobs (e.g. exports, imports)
expected_behaviour: GET /api/jobs lists jobs; GET /api/jobs/:id gets one; POST /api/jobs/:id/cancel cancels a running job

user_story: As a new user I check whether initial setup (AI provider, keys) is complete
expected_behaviour: GET /setup/status reports first-run/setup completeness for the wizard

user_story: As an analyst I apply changed .env values without restarting the server
expected_behaviour: POST /settings/ai-reload reloads DFIR_AI_* values from .env into process.env and reports them; POST /settings/reload reloads an allowlisted prefix of settings
test_result: covered by tests/server/*.test.ts (settings/ai-reload observed passing in companion test run)

user_story: As an analyst I see summary stats for a single case
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/stats returns case-level statistics distinct from the cross-case /disk-stats endpoint

user_story: As an analyst I export the case in a DFIR-IRIS-compatible format without pushing it live
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/iris-export produces the export payload; distinct from POST /cases/:id/iris-import which imports from a live IRIS instance

user_story: As an analyst I pause or resume AI processing for a case
expected_behaviour: GET/POST /cases/:id/ai-control; POST body {enabled, includeNotebook?}; disabling clears the pending capture buffer; re-enabling triggers gap backfill
test_result: covered by tests/analysis/aiControl.test.ts (2 tests, passing)

user_story: As an analyst I get AI-suggested remediation steps for the case
expected_behaviour: POST /cases/:id/remediation-plan returns an AI-generated remediation plan from current findings

user_story: As an analyst I monitor AI spend for a case
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/ai-cost returns accumulated AI usage/cost
test_result: covered by tests/server/aiCostRoute.test.ts (3 tests, passing)

user_story: As an analyst I track investigative hypotheses with status
expected_behaviour: GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE /cases/:id/hypotheses[/:hid]; POST requires non-empty title; PATCH 404 unknown id; status must be one of HYPOTHESIS_STATUSES; 501 if hypothesisStore unwired

user_story: As an analyst I set a minimum confidence threshold to filter noisy findings
expected_behaviour: GET/PUT /cases/:id/confidence-control; minConfidence must be 0-100 or null
test_result: covered by tests/analysis/confidenceControl.test.ts (4 tests, passing)

user_story: As an analyst on mobile I get a condensed case summary
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/mobile-summary returns a lightweight summary for the mobile view

user_story: As an analyst I present case findings live to stakeholders
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/presentation, /present, /present/export drive a presentation-mode view/export of the case

user_story: As an analyst I see statistically unusual activity flagged for review
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/anomalies returns derived anomaly candidates

user_story: As an analyst I see which hosts are most implicated
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/host-ranking returns hosts ranked by involvement (derived)

user_story: As an analyst I get defensive countermeasure suggestions mapped to detected techniques
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/d3fend-countermeasures; GET /cases/:id/attack-mitigations returns ATT&CK mitigations for detected techniques

user_story: As an analyst I visualize IOC/event geography on a map
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/geo-map and /geo-map.csv return geolocated points derived from IOCs/events

user_story: As an analyst I see estimated attacker dwell-time windows
expected_behaviour: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /cases/:id/dwell-windows manages derived/analyst-edited dwell windows
test_result: covered by tests/analysis/dwellWindow.test.ts (5 tests, passing)

user_story: As an analyst I search captured screenshot text
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/ocr-search full-text searches OCR-indexed capture content

user_story: As an analyst I mark a finding/event as a false positive with a reason
expected_behaviour: GET/POST /cases/:id/false-positive (400 if ref empty or reason=other lacks a note; optional addToWhitelist promotes the IOC; triggers re-synthesis); POST .../batch dedupes and re-synthesizes once; POST .../remove undoes a marking; POST .../suggest returns AI-assisted similar-item suggestions (404 if anchor not found, results re-validated against the real candidate pool to prevent hallucinated ids)

user_story: As an analyst I pin key findings to the top of the report
expected_behaviour: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /cases/:id/pinned-findings manages a manually ordered pin list

user_story: As an analyst I drop files into a watched folder for automatic import
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/drop-status shows pending drop-folder files; POST /cases/:id/drop/run-pending processes them

user_story: As an analyst I run a startup preflight check and control diagnostic behavior
expected_behaviour: GET /diagnostics/preflight runs environment/dependency checks; GET/POST /diagnostics/control toggles diagnostic settings

user_story: As an analyst I save a custom panel layout as a named view
expected_behaviour: GET/POST/DELETE /dashboard-views persists custom dashboard layouts

user_story: As an analyst I trace where an IOC came from
expected_behaviour: GET /ioc-sources lists configured sources; GET /ioc-provenance and /ioc-provenance-chain trace an IOC back through its import/enrichment history
test_result: covered by tests/analysis/iocProvenance.test.ts (4 tests, passing)

user_story: As an analyst I build a unified mega-timeline across all evidence sources
expected_behaviour: GET /cases/:id/super-timeline and /super-timeline.jsonl return the merged timeline; POST .../label and .../promote let an analyst label/promote raw super-timeline rows into the case timeline

user_story: As an analyst I run a configured external forensic tool against a file and ingest its output
expected_behaviour: GET /tools/status, POST /tools/reconnect, POST /cases/:id/tools/:toolId/run (501 if toolRunner unconfigured, 400 unknown tool/missing path, 404 unknown case); POST .../run-upload stages an uploaded file to a per-request temp dir, cleans up after run

user_story: As an analyst I refresh the detection-rule set used by an integrated tool
expected_behaviour: POST /cases/:id/tools/update-rules runs the configured update-rules command and returns its output; does not touch case data

user_story: As an analyst I register a custom external tool integration
expected_behaviour: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /tools/custom manages global custom-tool definitions; 501 if store unwired

user_story: As an analyst I suppress low-severity noise from external tool ingestion
expected_behaviour: GET/PUT /cases/:id/forensic-gate; minSeverity must be one of 5 severities or null
test_result: covered by tests/analysis/forensicGateControl.test.ts (3 tests, passing)

user_story: As an analyst I import Velociraptor results collected outside this session
expected_behaviour: POST /velociraptor/import-external ingests externally-collected VQL/hunt results

user_story: As a third-party system I push arbitrary evidence into a case via API key
expected_behaviour: POST /cases/:id/push authenticates via X-DFIR-Key or Bearer against a global DFIR_PUSH_TOKEN and/or per-case token (401/403 on failure); 404 unknown case; 400 on empty/undetected payload type; 501 if CSV/log payload but no AI provider configured; responds 202 and imports in background

user_story: As an analyst working in a DFIR console (Splunk, Velociraptor, Security Onion, SO-CRATES, Elastic/Kibana, CrowdStrike Falcon, VolWeb) I click a floating push button so the tool's actual result rows land in the case timeline
expected_behaviour: adapterForUrl() auto-detects the console by host/path/port; pageHook.ts intercepts matching fetch/XHR API responses; each adapter's extractRows() normalizes tool-specific JSON; falls back to DOM table scrape if nothing was intercepted; push disabled until a case is selected; unrecognized/invalid URLs -> no button; a misbehaving adapter matcher is caught so it cannot break others
test_result: extension/tests/adapters.test.ts (71 tests) + domTable.test.ts (7 tests), all passing

user_story: As an analyst on a page the extension misdetects, I force a specific adapter or force screenshot-only capture from the popup
expected_behaviour: resolveActiveAdapter(): empty = auto, OVERRIDE_NONE = force off, else forces the chosen adapter id but falls back to auto-detect if the id is stale/invalid; session-only, resets on navigation; popup row hides if content script unreachable (chrome:// pages, pre-install tabs)
test_result: extension/tests/override.test.ts (4 tests, passing); popup-status wiring untested

user_story: As an analyst I right-click selected text, a link, or a table anywhere on the web and choose Send to DFIR-Companion, without needing a recognized console adapter
expected_behaviour: contextMenuCapture.ts walks up from the captured contextmenu-event element to the nearest table ancestor (Chrome gives no cursor-element API); no table under cursor -> toast No table found; empty selection/rows -> toast Nothing to send; unreachable tab -> toast silently skipped (push already completed server-side)
test_result: no dedicated test file exists; only pure helpers (matrixToRows/buildArtifactFilename) are covered elsewhere

user_story: As an analyst working when the companion server is unreachable, my captures are not lost — they queue locally and flush automatically once the companion is back online
expected_behaviour: captureQueue.ts (IndexedDB) distinguishes transient failure (network error/5xx -> enqueue) from a hard rejection (4xx e.g. case missing/closed -> do not queue, amber badge); drains oldest-first, stops on first failure to preserve order, guards against concurrent drains
test_result: extension/tests/captureQueue.test.ts (4 tests) + captureController.test.ts (4 tests), all passing

user_story: As an analyst I drag the floating push button to a spot that does not obscure the console UI, and it stays there across reloads/tabs
expected_behaviour: position stored in chrome.storage.local; clamped into viewport on every re-injection (handles SPA re-renders and window resizes); drag past a 4px threshold suppresses the trailing click so it does not fire a spurious push
test_result: extension/tests/buttonPosition.test.ts (9 tests, passing); browser drag glue itself untested

user_story: As an analyst I pick an existing case from a dropdown to attach the extension to it, with refresh and open-dashboard shortcuts
expected_behaviour: popup.ts loadCases() populates from GET /cases (cases are only created in the dashboard); falls back to the last-used case id marked offline - last used when companion is unreachable or lacks GET /cases
test_result: DOM-driven popup flow has no dedicated test file

user_story: As an analyst I configure the companion URL, capture interval, and dedup threshold from the extension options page or popup
expected_behaviour: normalizeCompanionUrl() strips trailing slashes/whitespace, falls back to http://127.0.0.1:4773 default
test_result: extension/tests/settings.test.ts (8 tests, passing) covers the pure normalizer; UI wiring untested

feature: Toolbar status badge and hotkey display
user_story: As an analyst I glance at the toolbar icon/badge to see recording/idle/queued-count/rejected-case state, and see or rebind the toggle-capture hotkey
expected_behaviour: serviceWorker.ts/actionIcon.ts update badge/icon on state changes; popup showHotkey() reads the actual bound shortcut (may be unset if it conflicted at install) and links to chrome://extensions/shortcuts
test_result: extension/tests/manifest.test.ts only checks the command is declared, not the runtime badge/hotkey display logic
